platformsAndBuyingGuide.title: Call of Duty Black Ops II Platforms and Buying Guide
platformsAndBuyingGuide.subtitle: Where to Buy and Play Black Ops II
platformsAndBuyingGuide.intro: Call of Duty: Black Ops II is available on modern PlayStation consoles through its 2026 digital release, on Xbox through backward compatibility, and on PC through Steam. The core game includes Campaign, Multiplayer, and Zombies, while additional Zombies and Multiplayer maps are sold through separate DLC packs or the Season Pass.

items.howToBuy: Purchase the digital PS4 and PS5 product from the PlayStation Store.
items.version: Official PS5-compatible release launched on July 9, 2026.
items.onlineRequirements: PlayStation Plus and an Activision account are required for online Multiplayer. Local play supports one or two players.
items.includedContent: The original Campaign, Multiplayer, and Zombies experience. Revolution, Uprising, Vengeance, Apocalypse, and Nuketown Zombies are available through the separate Season Pass.
items.importantNotes: This is a release of the original Black Ops II experience rather than a new remake. Cross-platform Multiplayer is not supported.

items.howToBuy: Purchase the digital PS4 and PS5 product from the PlayStation Store.
items.version: Official PS4 release launched on July 9, 2026.
items.onlineRequirements: PlayStation Plus and an Activision account are required for online Multiplayer. Local play supports one or two players.
items.includedContent: Campaign, Multiplayer, and the original Zombies content. Additional map packs and Zombies maps require the Season Pass or individual DLC purchases.
items.importantNotes: Online play supports up to 18 players. Cross-platform Multiplayer is not supported.

items.howToBuy: Buy the digital Xbox 360 version from the Xbox Store. Xbox Series X can also use a compatible physical Xbox 360 disc, while Xbox Series S requires a digital license.
items.version: The Xbox 360 release runs through Xbox backward compatibility.
items.onlineRequirements: Online console Multiplayer requires an Xbox Game Pass plan that includes online Multiplayer access.
items.includedContent: Campaign, Multiplayer, and Zombies are included in the base game. The Season Pass and individual DLC packs remain separate purchases.
items.importantNotes: The game runs inside the Xbox 360 backward-compatible environment rather than as a native Series X|S version.

items.howToBuy: Buy the digital Xbox 360 version or insert a compatible physical Black Ops II disc.
items.version: Supported through Xbox One backward compatibility since April 2017.
items.onlineRequirements: Online console Multiplayer requires an Xbox Game Pass plan that includes online Multiplayer access.
items.includedContent: The complete base-game Campaign, Multiplayer, and Zombies modes. DLC maps require separate ownership.
items.importantNotes: Previously purchased compatible Xbox 360 digital content can be downloaded through the same Xbox account.

items.howToBuy: Purchase and install the game through Steam.
items.onlineRequirements: Steam and an internet connection are required for activation. Broadband internet is required for Multiplayer, while Steam Offline Mode can be used for supported single-player content after activation.
items.includedContent: The Steam base game provides access to the core Campaign, Multiplayer, and Zombies components. DLC map packs and the Season Pass are sold separately.
items.importantNotes: Minimum requirements include Windows Vista SP2 or Windows 7, 2 GB of RAM on 32-bit systems or 4 GB on 64-bit systems, a DirectX 9-compatible graphics card, and approximately 16 GB of storage.

beginnerGuide.title: Call of Duty Black Ops II Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.subtitle: Black Ops II Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.intro: Black Ops II contains a choice-driven Campaign, competitive Multiplayer, and round-based Zombies. Start with one mode, learn its basic movement and economy, and then add advanced systems such as Pick 10 classes, Scorestreaks, perks, and Pack-a-Punch.

steps.title: Choose Your First Mode
steps.summary: Select Campaign, Multiplayer, or Zombies based on what you want to learn first.
details: Choose Campaign on Recruit difficulty to learn movement, aiming, equipment, and weapon handling.
Choose Team Deathmatch in Multiplayer for a simple mode focused on eliminating the opposing team.
Choose Town Survival in Zombies for a compact practice area, or TranZit for the complete bus-route experience.
steps.quickTip: Campaign is the easiest place to adjust controls without competing against other players.

steps.title: Configure Controls and Visibility
steps.summary: Set a comfortable sensitivity and make important visual and audio information easy to notice.
details: Practice aiming down sights, firing, reloading, sprinting, jumping, crouching, going prone, and using equipment.
Begin with a low or medium look sensitivity and raise it gradually when turning starts to feel too slow.
Adjust brightness until dark areas remain visible without making bright environments appear washed out.
Use headphones or clear stereo audio to identify footsteps, gunfire, and nearby Zombies.
steps.quickTip: Change one setting at a time and test it for a full mission or match before changing it again.

steps.title: Complete the Campaign Carefully
steps.summary: Campaign decisions, optional objectives, and Strike Force missions can change later events.
details: Follow the objective marker, but search nearby rooms for alternate weapons and collectible intelligence.
Review the available loadout before each mission and bring weapons suited to the expected range.
Pay attention to major decisions because character outcomes and the ending can change.
Complete available Strike Force missions before they expire because their results affect the wider story.
steps.quickTip: Use cover and controlled bursts instead of firing continuously at distant enemies.

steps.title: Learn Multiplayer Objectives
steps.summary: Kills are useful, but objective actions also award score and help unlock Scorestreak rewards.
details: Start with Team Deathmatch, Kill Confirmed, or Domination before moving into modes with limited lives.
Watch the minimap for friendly positions, recent enemy fire, and open routes.
Capture objectives, collect tags, and assist teammates to build Scorestreak progress faster.
Dying resets progress toward the next Scorestreak, but rewards already earned remain available until used.
steps.quickTip: Move between cover points and check common doorways before entering open areas.

steps.title: Build a Simple Pick 10 Class
steps.summary: Create-a-Class unlocks at Multiplayer Level 4 and lets you spend ten allocation points on weapons, attachments, perks, and equipment.
details: Begin with one primary weapon, one useful attachment, three perks, and a small amount of equipment.
Choose an assault rifle for balanced medium-range fights or an SMG for close-range movement.
Do not equip a secondary weapon or extra equipment unless it supports your intended playstyle.
Use Wildcards later when you understand which extra attachment or perk is worth the additional Pick 10 point.
steps.quickTip: A simple class with fewer specialized items is easier to learn than a class built around several Wildcards.

steps.title: Understand Zombies Points and Survival
steps.summary: Damage and kills earn points that can be spent on doors, weapons, perks, the Mystery Box, and Pack-a-Punch.
details: Use early rounds to earn points before opening every available door.
Buy a dependable wall weapon so ammunition remains easier to replace.
Prioritize Jugger-Nog when the map provides access to it, then establish a safe movement route.
Keep moving in open areas and group Zombies into a train rather than allowing them to surround you.
Pack-a-Punch costs 5,000 points after the machine has been unlocked on the selected map.
steps.quickTip: Leave one slow Zombie alive near the end of a round when your team needs time to buy equipment or complete an objective.

steps.title: Progress Without Wasting Unlocks
steps.summary: Player levels unlock equipment, while individual weapon experience unlocks attachments and camouflage challenges.
details: Use one or two weapons consistently until their most useful attachments are unlocked.
Test new weapons in lower-pressure matches before placing them in a competitive class.
Choose Scorestreaks that you can earn regularly instead of selecting only the most expensive rewards.
After reaching the level cap, Prestige resets most normal progression while providing long-term progression rewards.
steps.quickTip: Reliable low-cost Scorestreaks are more useful to a beginner than rewards that rarely become available.

zombiesMapsAndEasterEggs.title: Call of Duty Black Ops II Zombies Maps and Easter Eggs
zombiesMapsAndEasterEggs.subtitle: Zombies Maps, Easter Eggs, and Buildables
zombiesMapsAndEasterEggs.intro: Black Ops II Zombies expands from the large TranZit bus route into vertical skyscrapers, Alcatraz Island, an underground western town, and a World War I battlefield. Each DLC map introduces new movement systems, buildable equipment, and a longer cooperative quest.

maps.name: TranZit
maps.contentAccess: Included with the base game as part of Green Run.
maps.setting: A post-apocalyptic bus route connecting the Bus Depot, Diner, Farm, Power Station, and Town.
mainObjectives: Use the bus or dangerous fog routes to travel between locations.
Turn on the power at the Power Station.
Build equipment from parts found throughout the route.
Prepare the radio towers for the Tower of Babble quest.
maps.packAPunch: Open the Town bank vault, keep the hidden power-station door energized with a Turbine, and enter the underground laboratory below the bank.
wonderWeapons: Thrustodyne Aeronautics Model 23, commonly called the Jet Gun
maps.mainQuest: Tower of Babble offers separate Maxis and Richtofen paths centered on powering and activating the transmission tower.
maps.recommendedFor: Players ready to learn large-map navigation, buildable parts, and coordinated objectives.

maps.name: Nuketown Zombies
maps.contentAccess: Available separately and included with the Black Ops II Season Pass.
maps.setting: A compact nuclear-test neighborhood based on the classic Nuketown Multiplayer map.
mainObjectives: Survive in a small area with limited escape routes.
Listen for falling machines as perks and Pack-a-Punch arrive during the match.
Adapt to the random order in which the machines appear.
maps.packAPunch: The machine descends onto the map in a random sequence alongside the perk machines as rounds advance.
wonderWeapons: Ray Gun Mark II when the compatible DLC content is installed
maps.mainQuest: Nuketown Zombies does not contain a full multi-step main Easter egg quest.
maps.recommendedFor: New players practicing movement, ammunition management, and basic round survival.

maps.name: Die Rise
maps.contentAccess: Included in the Revolution DLC pack and Season Pass.
maps.setting: Collapsed skyscrapers in a ruined Chinese city, connected by elevators, rooftops, and dangerous vertical drops.
mainObjectives: Turn on the power inside the main building complex.
Use elevators and rooftop jumps to move between separated areas.
Build the Sliquifier and Trample Steam.
Coordinate the rooftop symbols and towers for the main quest.
maps.packAPunch: Pack-a-Punch travels on an elevator, requiring players to monitor elevator positions and avoid being trapped when it moves.
maps.mainQuest: High Maintenance has Maxis and Richtofen paths and requires a coordinated four-player team.
maps.recommendedFor: Experienced players comfortable with vertical movement, narrow routes, and fall hazards.

maps.name: Mob of the Dead
maps.contentAccess: Included in the Uprising DLC pack and Season Pass.
maps.setting: Alcatraz Island during the 1930s, featuring four imprisoned mobsters trapped in a repeating supernatural cycle.
mainObjectives: Enter Afterlife to power devices and open inaccessible areas.
Collect plane parts throughout the prison.
Build the plane on the roof and fly to the Golden Gate Bridge.
Repeat the escape cycle and complete the final confrontation.
maps.packAPunch: Build and fly the plane to the Golden Gate Bridge, where the Pack-a-Punch machine is located.
maps.mainQuest: Pop Goes the Weasel follows the prisoners' repeated escape attempts and ends with a choice between continuing or breaking the cycle.
maps.recommendedFor: Players seeking a story-focused map with Afterlife puzzles, strong atmosphere, and a structured cooperative quest.

maps.name: Buried
maps.contentAccess: Included in the Vengeance DLC pack and Season Pass.
maps.setting: An underground Old West town containing a saloon, bank, courthouse, haunted mansion, and hedge maze.
mainObjectives: Free Arthur from his cell and use candy or booze to control his actions.
Use Arthur to break barriers, move objects, and assist with buildables.
Pass through the haunted mansion and maze.
Coordinate switches, targets, and time-sensitive tasks for the main quest.
maps.packAPunch: Travel through the haunted mansion, cross the hedge maze, and descend beneath the gazebo.
maps.mainQuest: Mined Games contains Maxis and Richtofen paths and requires coordinated four-player puzzle solving.
maps.recommendedFor: Players learning advanced buildables and Easter eggs on a map with generous banking and movement options.

maps.name: Origins
maps.contentAccess: Included in the Apocalypse DLC pack and Season Pass.
maps.setting: An alternate World War I battlefield with trenches, giant robots, an excavation site, and ancient underground chambers.
mainObjectives: Activate all six generators.
Collect gramophone records and open the elemental tunnels.
Build and upgrade the four Elemental Staffs.
Complete the Little Lost Girl quest steps while surviving Panzer Soldat attacks.
maps.packAPunch: Activate all six generators to unlock Pack-a-Punch at the Excavation Site.
maps.mainQuest: Little Lost Girl combines upgraded staffs, robot access, special melee challenges, and coordinated final objectives.
maps.recommendedFor: Advanced teams ready for the most system-heavy and objective-focused Black Ops II Zombies map.

weaponsTierList.title: Call of Duty Black Ops II Weapons Tier List
weaponsTierList.subtitle: Black Ops II Weapons Tier List
weaponsTierList.intro: The best Black Ops II weapon depends on map size and playstyle. S-tier weapons deliver exceptional performance in their intended role, A-tier choices remain highly competitive with manageable drawbacks, and B-tier weapons are dependable options that require more specialized positioning.

tiers.label: Top-Tier Choices
tiers.description: Weapons with exceptional damage, handling, or role efficiency across common Multiplayer situations.

weapons.name: AN-94
weapons.strengths: The first two rounds fire faster than the remaining shots, creating a powerful opening burst with manageable recoil.
weapons.weaknesses: Sustained fire becomes slower after the initial rounds, so missed opening shots reduce its advantage.
weapons.playstyle: Controlled bursts, lane control, and accurate objective defense.

weapons.name: M8A1
weapons.strengths: Fast four-round bursts, low recoil, and excellent accuracy can eliminate targets quickly when the full burst connects.
weapons.weaknesses: Missing a burst creates a delay before the next shot sequence and makes close-range panic fights difficult.
weapons.playstyle: Precision aiming, head-glitch positions, and controlled map lanes.

weapons.name: MSMC
weapons.strengths: High close-range damage, strong mobility, and controllable recoil make it highly effective for aggressive movement.
weapons.weaknesses: Damage falls noticeably at distance and the magazine is less forgiving during fights against multiple players.
weapons.playstyle: Flanking, objective rushing, and fast close-quarters engagements.

weapons.name: R870 MCS
weapons.strengths: Reliable close-range one-shot potential and strong effective reach for a pump-action shotgun.
weapons.weaknesses: A missed shot leaves the user exposed during the pump animation.
weapons.playstyle: Room clearing, corner control, and short-route objective defense.

weapons.name: DSR 50
weapons.strengths: Powerful one-shot capability and strong performance when holding long sightlines.
weapons.weaknesses: Slow aiming and bolt-cycling speed punish missed shots and aggressive close-range movement.
weapons.playstyle: Defensive sniping, overwatch, and long-lane picks.

tiers.label: Strong Competitive Weapons
tiers.description: Highly effective weapons with one notable handling, ammunition, or consistency limitation.

weapons.name: SCAR-H
weapons.strengths: High damage and dependable range allow it to win fights with fewer accurate shots.
weapons.weaknesses: A 20-round magazine and noticeable recoil require careful ammunition control.
weapons.playstyle: Methodical movement, controlled bursts, and defensive engagements.

weapons.name: FAL OSW
weapons.strengths: Heavy semi-automatic damage rewards accurate follow-up shots and disciplined trigger speed.
weapons.weaknesses: Repeated firing can increase recoil, while missed shots are harder to recover from at close range.
weapons.playstyle: Precision shooting and controlled lane coverage.

weapons.name: PDW-57
weapons.strengths: A large 50-round magazine supports sustained pressure, multiple eliminations, and aggressive objective play.
weapons.weaknesses: Lower damage at longer ranges makes it less effective against accurate assault rifles.
weapons.playstyle: Continuous rushing, objective pressure, and extended close-range fights.

weapons.name: Skorpion EVO
weapons.strengths: Extremely high fire rate creates outstanding close-range elimination speed.
weapons.weaknesses: Rapid ammunition consumption and stronger recoil reduce its value outside close quarters.
weapons.playstyle: Fast flanking, hip-fire engagements, and indoor combat.

weapons.name: LSAT
weapons.strengths: Stable recoil, a large magazine, and dependable ranged damage make it effective for sustained fire.
weapons.weaknesses: Slow aiming, movement, and reload speeds make repositioning difficult.
weapons.playstyle: Support fire, objective defense, and locking down sightlines.

weapons.name: KSG
weapons.strengths: Fires a precise slug with one-shot potential at distances beyond normal shotgun range.
weapons.weaknesses: The single projectile demands accurate aim and missed shots are heavily punished.
weapons.playstyle: Precision shotgun play and carefully aimed doorway control.

weapons.name: Ballista
weapons.strengths: Faster handling and bolt cycling than the DSR 50 support a more mobile sniping style.
weapons.weaknesses: Its smaller reliable one-shot area requires better upper-body accuracy.
weapons.playstyle: Aggressive sniping, quick repositioning, and fast follow-up shots.

tiers.label: Reliable and Specialized
tiers.description: Useful weapons that remain effective when matched with the correct range, attachments, and playstyle.

weapons.name: M27
weapons.strengths: Very low recoil and clear iron sights make it one of the easiest rifles to control.
weapons.weaknesses: Lower per-shot damage can result in slower eliminations against stronger rifles.
weapons.playstyle: Beginner-friendly accuracy, support fire, and consistent ranged tracking.

weapons.name: MP7
weapons.strengths: Predictable recoil, good handling, and a comfortable fire rate make it dependable at several ranges.
weapons.weaknesses: It lacks the close-range power of the MSMC and the extreme fire rate of the Skorpion EVO.
weapons.playstyle: Mobile all-purpose play and controlled flanking.

weapons.name: HAMR
weapons.strengths: A fast opening fire rate, strong damage, and a large magazine provide excellent initial pressure.
weapons.weaknesses: Its fire rate changes during sustained fire and its heavy handling slows aggressive movement.
weapons.playstyle: Pre-aimed defense, suppressive fire, and holding objectives.

weapons.name: MK 48
weapons.strengths: Strong ranged damage and a large ammunition supply support long defensive engagements.
weapons.weaknesses: Slow movement, aiming, and reload speed require the user to prepare positions in advance.
weapons.playstyle: Long-lane defense and sustained team support.

weapons.name: B23R
weapons.strengths: Rapid three-round bursts can eliminate nearby enemies quickly when the full burst connects.
weapons.weaknesses: Burst recoil and frequent ammunition use make repeated distant shots inconsistent.
weapons.playstyle: Emergency secondary use and fast close-range finishes.

weapons.name: Tac-45
weapons.strengths: High semi-automatic damage makes it a powerful backup weapon at close range.
weapons.weaknesses: Its smaller magazine requires accurate shots and careful reload timing.
weapons.playstyle: Reliable secondary use, finishing damaged enemies, and lightweight classes.

faq.title: Call of Duty Black Ops II
faq.subtitle: Quick answers about Call of Duty Black Ops II platforms, modes, and content

questions.question: What platforms is Call of Duty Black Ops II available on?
questions.answer: Call of Duty Black Ops II is available on PS5 and PS4 through the 2026 digital release, on Xbox Series X|S and Xbox One through backward compatibility, and on PC through Steam.

questions.question: Does Call of Duty Black Ops II support cross-platform multiplayer?
questions.answer: No. Call of Duty Black Ops II does not support cross-platform multiplayer. Online play matches players on the same platform family, and the PS4/PS5 release supports up to 18 online players.

questions.question: What modes are included in Call of Duty Black Ops II?
questions.answer: Call of Duty Black Ops II includes the branching Campaign, competitive Multiplayer with Pick 10 classes and Scorestreaks, and round-based Zombies. Additional maps arrive through DLC packs and the Season Pass.

questions.question: Is the 2026 Call of Duty Black Ops II PlayStation release a remake?
questions.answer: No. The 2026 PlayStation release is a port of the original Call of Duty Black Ops II experience, not a remake. The core Campaign, Multiplayer, and Zombies content remains the same.

questions.question: Do I need PlayStation Plus for Call of Duty Black Ops II multiplayer?
questions.answer: Yes. Online Multiplayer in Call of Duty Black Ops II on PlayStation requires a PlayStation Plus subscription and an Activision account. Local play supports one or two players without it.

questions.question: What DLC is available for Call of Duty Black Ops II?
questions.answer: Call of Duty Black Ops II DLC includes the Revolution, Uprising, Vengeance, and Apocalypse map packs. Each adds Multiplayer maps and Zombies content, and the Season Pass bundles them together.
